headline: CONFIRMATION. April 22 96-page Pentagon-rebuttal filing in DC Circuit appeal is a SUBSTANTIVE positive INT/ACC signal — Anthropic explicitly defends inability to manipulate Claude in classified DoD networks AND maintains refusal to permit 'all lawful purposes' use including mass domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons. Anthropic prevailed in parallel SF federal court case (stigmatizing labels removed). Trump administration drafting executive order to reintegrate Anthropic (Axios April 29 — out of window). April 22 SB3444 opposition (vs OpenAI support) further supports INT positioning. Net effect: position consistent with current 61.6 (Established band, +1.6 above 60-band boundary).

evidence.finding: 96-page DC Circuit appeals filing explicitly rebuts Pentagon supply-chain risk designation; argues Anthropic cannot manipulate Claude once deployed in classified networks; reiterates refusal to allow 'all lawful purposes' use including mass domestic surveillance + fully autonomous weapons; oral arguments May 19.
Confirms parallel SF federal court win removed stigmatizing labels; Pentagon dispute appears to be the outlier institutional position relative to NSA Mythos use, Trump's CNBC reconciliation comments.
Anthropic opposed Illinois SB3444 (AI liability shield) — divergent from OpenAI support; consistent with stated safety positioning.
NSA confirmed using Anthropic Mythos despite Pentagon dispute — INT mitigation factor (already in prior baseline).
